> Assembled by Clarify from confirmed outputs. Items marked `ASSUMPTION` /
> `OPEN QUESTION` are unresolved. Status is never `Approved` while a blocker-level
> finding exists.

## Executive summary
A short read for the Business Owner / Approver before the detail. Compose from the
sections below — do not introduce new claims.
- Problem / opportunity: <…>
- Target users / audience: <…>
- Scope of this phase: <…>
- Expected business outcome: <…>
- Key open items / blockers: <…> (full list in §14–§15)

## 1. Business context & problem statement
<Why this initiative exists: the business problem, current pain, opportunity.>

## 2. Business objectives & success criteria
- Objective: <…>
- Measurable success criterion / KPI: <…>

## 3. Scope
**In scope**
- <…>

**Out of scope**
- <…>

## 4. Stakeholders
| Stakeholder | Role / interest | Responsibility (RACI) |
| --- | --- | --- |

## 5. Business requirements
High-level, outcome-oriented (the *what* and *why*, not the *how*).
| BReq id | Business requirement | Priority | Rationale | Source |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BR-1 | <the business needs …> | <M/S/C> | <why it matters> | <…> |

## 6. Business rules & policies
- BR1: <explicit policy / rule>  — or `OPEN QUESTION: <unknown rule>`

## 7. Functional Flows (process-centric)
Organized **by business process** (use case), not by diagram type. Each flow keeps
its step-by-step, activity, and sequence together for the SAME process. Composed
from `model-suggestions.md`; do not invent steps/rules/errors.

### 7.1 Customer/User Journey Overview
<High-level end-to-end journey only: entry → selection → data entry →
preview/confirmation → authentication if applicable → processing → result →
detail. Detail lives in the per-flow blocks below. Mark missing steps `OPEN QUESTION`.>

### 7.2 Flow Catalog
| Flow ID | Business name | Actor(s) | Goal | Diagrams | Related rules (BR) | Related error codes | Requirement (BRD-Rxx) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| F01 | <…> | <…> | <…> | activity + sequence | <…> | <…> | <BRD-R01> |

### 7.3 Flow F01 — <Business name>
**7.3.1 Step-by-step**
| # | Actor | Action | System response | Rule / validation / error code |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | <…> | <…> | <…> | <…> |

**7.3.2 Activity diagram (PlantUML)** — process flow + decisions
```plantuml
<from model-suggestions.md — THIS flow>
```
**View / edit:** https://www.plantuml.com/plantuml — ref: https://plantuml.com/

**7.3.3 Sequence diagram (Mermaid)** — system interaction for the **same** flow
```mermaid
<from model-suggestions.md — THIS flow>
```
**View / edit:** https://mermaid.live/
<!-- Simple/single-system flow: replace with "Sequence diagram not required — <reason>". -->

**7.3.4 Gaps revealed / Open questions**
- <gap or `OPEN QUESTION` from this flow>

### 7.4 Flow F02 — <Business name>
<repeat 7.x.1–7.x.4; activity and sequence in one block must be the SAME process —
never mix process A's activity with process B's sequence>

### 7.5 Screen / Display Matrix
Placed **after** the flows. Map each screen back to the flow/step it serves.
| Screen | Flow / Step | Purpose | Display fields | User actions | Validation | Error / empty / loading states | UX / content notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

### 7.6 Screen Wireframes (low-fidelity HTML)
Render an inline HTML visualization widget here when the target Markdown renderer
supports it; otherwise write `clarify-output/wireframes.html` and link it here.
Use `templates/wireframe-template.html`. The widget is grayscale, derive-only, and
maps every screen back to its source flow/step and requirement. Do not use ASCII
wireframes as the primary screen artifact.

## 8. Data & systems impact
<From api-data-impact.md when build-ready layer exists; otherwise summarize
business-level affected systems from the draft and mark detailed API/data impact
as optional for Dev/QA handoff.>

## 9. Edge cases, exception handling & messages
<Summary from edge-case-matrix.md.>

### 9.1 Error Handling & Message Mapping
Each error maps back to the flow/step it occurs in (from §7), so design/QA/CS do not
have to guess where it happens.
| Error code | Flow / Step | Scenario | Entity state | Transaction / operation state | User-facing message | Retryable? | Required action | Needs Ops/CS? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

### 9.2 State summary
| Model type | State set | Trigger / event | Owner system | Terminal states | Gaps |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Entity state | <from model-suggestions.md> | <who/what moves it> | <source of truth> | <…> | <…> |
| Transaction / operation state | <from model-suggestions.md> | <…> | <…> | <…> | <…> |

## 10. Constraints & dependencies
- Constraint: <regulatory / budget / timeline>
- Dependency: <…>

## 11. Risks & compliance
<From risk/compliance analysis: risk, impact, control, owner.>

## 12. Non-functional & regulatory requirements
- <security / privacy / availability / compliance (e.g. KYC, GDPR)>

## 13. Traceability summary
<Include when `traceability-matrix.md` exists. If not, state: "Detailed
story/AC/test traceability was not generated; run `/clarify:from-spec` only if
Dev/QA build-ready handoff is needed.">
Links each requirement to the flow that realizes it, its rule, and its error/state,
so an unlinked requirement (no flow/rule) is visible. Screen mapping optional.
| BReq id | Flow (Fxx) | Business rule | Error / state | Story id | AC | Test id |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

## 14. Open items
Grouped for reviewers. Items here are unresolved or proposed — not agreed scope.

### 14.1 Assumptions
- ASSUMPTION: <stated default in effect>

### 14.2 Open Questions
- OPEN QUESTION: <unresolved item>

### 14.3 Suggested additional capabilities (for confirmation)
- SUGGESTION: <capability the business need + domain implies but the doc omits> — rationale: <why>.

## 15. Sign-off blockers (must resolve before approval)
- OPEN QUESTION: <the blocker subset of §14.2>
- Blocker findings (from audit-report.md): <…>

## 16. Approval / sign-off
| Approver | Role | Decision | Date |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

## Appendix: Generated artifacts
Companion files produced by Clarify in `clarify-output/` (list only those that
exist; diagrams are **source code** rendered via the linked viewers — Clarify does
not produce image files).
| Artifact | Description | Path |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Draft | Shaped BRD draft | `clarify-output/brd-draft.md` |
| Audit report | Score, band, findings | `clarify-output/audit-report.md` |
| Edge-case matrix | Edge / negative / boundary / temporal / batch | `clarify-output/edge-case-matrix.md` |
| Error handling | Error → state → user-message → action map | `clarify-output/error-handling.md` |
| Model suggestions | Per-flow activity (PlantUML) + sequence (Mermaid) + state | `clarify-output/model-suggestions.md` |
| Wireframes | Low-fi HTML screen wireframes | `clarify-output/wireframes.html` |
| API/data impact | Entities, source of truth, endpoints | `clarify-output/api-data-impact.md` |
| Stories + AC | User stories and acceptance criteria | `clarify-output/stories.md` |
| Test scenarios | Test cases | `clarify-output/test-scenarios.md` |
| Traceability | Requirement ↔ flow ↔ rule ↔ error ↔ story ↔ test | `clarify-output/traceability-matrix.md` |
